commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer should decide the transient namespace reset and canonicalize this against the older overlapping Signal reaction PR; there is no narrow automated repair finding.; Cleared: The diff changes internal Signal approval-reaction plumbing and tests without adding dependencies, workflows, permissions, lockfiles, secret handling, or package execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
Moves Signal approval reaction hinting and binding from low-level outbound text parsing to structured approval payload rendering and after-delivery registration, with monitor-path and regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +121, Tests +591. Total +712 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows Signal reactions are appended and registered from approval-looking text in `sendMessageSignal`, and the PR adds source-level tests plus live Signal E2E proof for the structured after-delivery path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should decide the transient namespace reset and canonicalize this against the older overlapping Signal reaction PR; there is no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: Fix Signal approval reactions for structured delivery
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Signal approval reaction controls could be added or registered from the wrong delivery path. This made reaction behavior inconsistent across normal Signal sends, forwarded approval prompts, and monitor-delivered replies.

## Why This Change Was Made

This changes Signal approval reactions to follow the structured approval payload and actual delivered Signal message IDs. Reaction targets are now registered only after delivery, only for structured approval payloads, and only for the delivered message/chunk that visibly includes the reaction guidance.

Ordinary outbound text is sent unchanged, even if it contains approval command examples.

## User Impact

Signal users still get reaction shortcuts on supported approval prompts. Manual `/approve ...` replies continue to work.

During upgrade, in-flight Signal reaction shortcuts from earlier prompts may fall back to manual `/approve`; newly delivered prompts receive fresh reaction bindings.

The visible reaction instructions and the stored reaction binding now stay aligned, including for monitor replies and chunked Signal messages.

## Evidence

- `pnpm test extensions/signal`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:changed -- extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.ts extensions/signal/src/channel.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.ts extensions/signal/src/send.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.approval-reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts`
  - Testbox: `tbx_01kw0m8yp0fczw8wm4zzx5aapd`
- `$autoreview --mode local`
  - Clean: no accepted/actionable findings
- Live Signal E2E with two local test accounts:
  - ordinary non-approval message path did not create a reaction binding
  - structured approval prompt rendered visible reaction guidance
  - actual delivered Signal message ID resolved to the expected approval target
  - live reaction from the approver account completed successfully
target.author: joshavant
